title: [lichen_l2.h:40-42] CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS default in header bypasses Kconfig
description:
Gap found: Header-level defaults not reviewed in Round 1.

Why this was missed: Kconfig interaction patterns not examined.

The actual bug: Lines 40-42 define CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS with a default of 16 if not defined. This means the header provides a fallback default that may conflict with the Kconfig system. If a Kconfig file defines LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS without the CONFIG_ prefix, or if the Kconfig isn't processed before this header, the hardcoded 16 takes effect silently. Better practice: use BUILD_ASSERT to require the Kconfig value rather than defaulting, or move the default exclusively to Kconfig. This pattern appears in multiple LICHEN headers. Priority 4 because current builds process Kconfig first.
close_reason: Fixed: Removed header default for CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS. Kconfig in lichen/subsys/lichen/link/Kconfig provides the definitive default (16, range 4-64). LICHEN_L2 depends on LICHEN_LINK, guaranteeing the config is set. Header now documents the Kconfig source instead of providing a bypass.
